PyTorch默认norm='backward' (normalize by 1/n), while TensorFlow's tf.signal.ifft uses unnormalized IFFT (equivalent to norm='forward' in PyTorch but with no explicit norm param — it matches 'forward' only when PyTorch uses norm='forward'). However, the current PyTorch test uses norm='forward', but TF ifft is actually equivalent to PyTorch's norm='backward' for inverse consistency with its fft counterpart. To align semantics, PyTorch should use norm='backward' (default) to match TF's implicit normalization behavior.
